sections.paragraph:
Arab. سحط-

(1) to slay animals, Gen 37:31 Isa 22:13 especially a victim, Levit. 1:5 , 11 and even a human victim, Gen 22:10 Isa 57:5. Hos 5:2, שַׁחֲטָה שֵׂטִים הֶעֱמִיקוּ “in slaughtering (victims) they make deep their transgression.”

(2) to kill (persons), 2Ki 25:7 Jerem. 39:6 . חֵץ שֹׁוחֵט Jer 9:7, a deadly arrow. In קרי there is שָׁחוּט, the sense not being different, prop. made to kill.

(3) זָהָב שָׁחוּט 1Ki 10:16, 17 1Ki 10:17 2Ch 9:15, 16 2Ch 9:16, prob. mixed gold, alloyed with another metal, comp. Arab. سلحط to dilute wine with water; see more in Comment. on Isa 1:22. The LXX. understood gold beaten out, compare שָׁטַח to spread out, the letters being transposed.

Niphal, pass. of No. 1, Levit. 6:18 Num 11:22. Hence-
